concept: Autumn / Season of Harvest / Time of Letting Go
description: The season after summer when leaves fall and harvest occurs; time of completion and release. 'Muero' names the ripening season—fruits gathered, leaves falling, the world preparing for rest.
sound_symbolism: The word releases and settles. 'Mu' grounds with gathering presence, harvest collected. The vowel 'e' opens to completion. Then 'ro' rolls downward, leaves falling.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mua muero' (in autumn). Third of the four seasons. Time of harvest, gratitude, and preparation for winter.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Autumn determined winter's survival—the harvest stored would sustain life through cold months.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Autumn teaches letting go—the trees release their leaves without resistance, modeling non-attachment.
